pattern: avrdude: stk500_recv(): programmer is not responding
cause: Bootloader missing or corrupted, wrong board selected, or COM port issue
solution: Select correct board and COM port. Press reset button before uploading. Re-burn bootloader using ISP programmer. Check USB cable has data wires.

cause: Flash write failed, insufficient power, or corrupted bootloader
solution: Use a different USB port or cable. Ensure adequate power supply. Re-burn bootloader.
Enable compiler optimizations (-Os). Remove unused libraries. Use PROGMEM for constant data. Consider larger board.

cause: Missing header file, typo in type name, or missing library
solution: Include required header files. Check spelling and case. Install missing library via Library Manager.

pattern: was not declared in this scope
cause: Function or variable not declared, wrong scope, or typo
solution: Declare functions before use. Check variable scope. Verify function names match.

cause: Ticker library not installed or not included
solution: Install Ticker library via Library Manager: Sketch > Include Library > Ticker. Or use ESP32-specific Ticker.h

cause: I2C device not found or wrong address
solution: Run I2C scanner to find device address. Check connections. Add pull-up resistors (4.7K-10K). Verify device supports I2C.

pattern: SPI.h: No such file or directory
cause: SPI library not available for this board
solution: Ensure board supports SPI. Include SPI.h correctly. For ESP32, use SPI.h from Espressif framework.

pattern: low memory available, stability problems may occur
solution: Reduce buffer sizes. Avoid using String class. Move constants to PROGMEM. Use F() macro for Serial.print.
Scroll up in error output to find actual error message. Common causes: missing semicolons, undeclared variables, type mismatches.
Add const keyword or use strcpy(). Check function signature and parameter types.

pattern: collect2.exe: error: ld returned 1 exit status
cause: Linker error, duplicate symbols, or undefined reference
solution: Check for multiple definitions of functions/variables. Ensure all library source files are included. Remove conflicting library versions.

cause: setup() or loop() functions not defined
solution: Ensure setup() and loop() functions are defined in your sketch. Check for typos in function names.

cause: Board not connected, wrong port, or driver not installed
solution: Check USB connection. Install CH340/FTDI drivers. Select correct COM port. Try different USB cable.
This board uses Linux processor. Some libraries may not be compatible. Check library documentation for board support.
F() stores strings in flash memory: Serial.println(F("Hello")). Don't use F() with variables.

cause: Wrong pin mode not set, or ADC not configured
solution: Set pinMode(pin, INPUT) for digitalRead. For analogRead, ensure pin is ADC-capable. Check board pin capabilities.
Tone only works on pins with timer support (usually pins 3, 5, 6, 9, 10, 11 on Uno). Check board specifications.
